content: In this lecture, Ian Ball discusses infinitely repeated games, which are scenarios in which players engage in the same stage game indefinitely, allowing for cooperation that wouldn’t exist in one-shot games.
title: Lecture 13: Infinitely Repeated Games

video_metadata.video_tags: 14-12-economic-applications-of-game-theory-fall-2025, equilibrium, last period, prisoner's dilemma, subgame nash equilibrium
video_metadata.youtube_description:
MIT 14.12 Economic Applications of Game Theory, Fall 2025 
Instructor: Ian Ball 
View the complete course: https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/14-12-economic-applications-of-game-theory-fall-2025/
YouTube Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLUl4u3cNGP63quuKvMHCt3cmTmt0O2qpv
In this lecture, Ian Ball discusses infinitely repeated games, which are scenarios where players engage in the same stage game indefinitely, allowing for cooperation that wouldn't exist in one-shot games.
